title: Berry-Flux Diagnosis Of Node Charge
summary: To determine the charge of an isolated Weyl node, surround it by a small sphere in momentum space, compute the Berry curvature of the occupied state, and integrate the flux over the sphere.
assumptions: A local occupied-state bundle can be constructed on the surrounding sphere.
regime: Local diagnostic for an isolated point node.
scope: Operational procedure distilled from Lecture One.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One states the flux rule explicitly.

representation: Local computational diagnostic using curvature flux.
failure_modes: The method must be modified for non-abelian occupied subspaces.
retrieval_hints: Use as an explicit procedure rather than only a conceptual statement.
